borrowed.note: Used persistent, non-focus-stealing alert treatment for important recovery state that should not interrupt active work.

borrowed.appliedTo: detail-drawer labeling and focus return rules
borrowed.note: Borrowed focus and labelling discipline for the detail drawer without turning the route into a detached modal flow.
Used show-hide discipline for bounded reveal regions rather than stacking multiple always-open recovery blocks.
Used as the basis for narrow-layout reflow and to avoid horizontal clipping in the exception workspace.

borrowed.appliedTo: sticky rails and detail drawer behavior
borrowed.note: Used to keep sticky shell chrome and the exception drawer from hiding focused controls.

borrowed.appliedTo: callback pending and recovery status copy
borrowed.note: Used to keep non-modal state changes explicit without moving focus.

borrowed.appliedTo: urgent bounce-back copy and action hierarchy
borrowed.note: Borrowed brief, high-signal copy and a single dominant next step for unusual or risky actions.
Borrowed concise receipt hierarchy while keeping the recovery state visibly unresolved when supervision still blocks closure.
Used stable selectors and route-level assertions for shell continuity and recovery state.
Used separate contexts for layout and reduced-motion checks.

borrowed.appliedTo: accessibility assertions for recovery and exception regions
borrowed.note: Used for structured accessibility snapshots of the case recovery canvas and exception workspace.

borrowed.appliedTo: desktop, tablet, and narrow visual baselines
borrowed.note: Used for stable evidence captures of recovery posture across breakpoints.
Used to keep navigation and selection regressions reviewable.
Borrowed the emphasis on hierarchy stress tests and low-noise chrome, not Linear’s visual styling.
Used only as secondary inspiration for keeping the exception workspace structured and muscle-memory friendly.

rejected.pattern: Generic ticketing inbox with detached incident pages
rejected.reason: Conflicts with same-shell recovery and loses the active case anchor.

rejected.pattern: Alarm-red dashboard counters and repeated warning banners
rejected.reason: Creates alert fatigue and hides the single next safe action.
Breaks provenance visibility and encourages detached state transitions.
